rationale: The citing paper uses McAlister et al. (2016) for more than background listing. It positions that study in Table 1 as prior evidence that advertising effectiveness for shareholder value depends on a contingency (firm strategy), and then develops its own contingency framework (firm scope plus receivables). It also borrows the industry-relative advertising spending operationalization and cites McAlister et al. to justify the exogeneity logic of spending-share measures, which affects core model specification.
relation_description: The citing paper applies methodological and framing elements from McAlister et al. (2016), especially the use of industry-relative advertising investment and a contingency view of advertising effectiveness for firm-value outcomes. It transfers these ideas to a new setting that jointly models advertising with receivables and tests moderation by firm scope.
